heading: National registry of Korean American divided families
label: 7818 National registry of Korean American divided families
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2026, and not as part of the North Korean Human Rights Act of 2004, which comprises this chapter.

Consultations on Reuniting Korean Americans With Family Members in North Korea, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–263, div. E, title LV, § 5599B136 Stat. 3400Dec. 23, 2022“(a)Consultations.—“(1).—Consultations with south koreaThe Secretary of State, or a designee of the Secretary, should consult with officials of South Korea, as appropriate, on potential opportunities to reunite Korean American families with family members in North Korea from which such Korean American families were divided after the signing of the Korean War Armistice Agreement, including potential opportunities for video reunions for Korean Americans with such family members.“(2).—Consultations with korean americansThe Special Envoy on North Korean Human Rights Issues of the Department of State should regularly consult with representatives of Korean Americans who have family members in North Korea with respect to efforts to reunite families divided after the signing of the Korean War Armistice Agreement, including potential opportunities for video reunions for Korean Americans with such family members.“(b).—ReportNot later than 120 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [], and annually thereafter for three years, the Secretary of State, acting through the Special Envoy on North Korean Human Rights Issues or other appropriate designee, shall submit to the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate a report on the consultations conducted pursuant to this section during the preceding year.”Dec. 23, 2022

children.label: A
children.text: engage, to the extent practicable, Korean American families who wish to be reunited with family members residing in North Korea from which such Korean American families were divided after the signing of the Agreement Concerning a Military Armistice in Korea, signed at Panmunjom (commonly referred to as the “Korean War Armistice Agreement” ), in anticipation of future reunions for such families and family members, including in-person and video reunions; andJuly 27, 1953

children.label: i
children.text: to facilitate such future reunions; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to provide for a repository of information about such Korean American families and family members in North Korea, including information about individuals who may be deceased.

children.label: A
children.text: the United States person whose personally identifiable information would be disclosed as a result of an agreement has provided consent to such disclosure; and

children.label: ii
children.text: will not be disclosed improperly to other parties outside the agreement.

children.heading: Actions to facilitate dialogue between the United States and North Korea
children.label: b Actions to facilitate dialogue between the United States and North Korea

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary of State should take steps to ensure that any direct dialogue between the United States and North Korea includes progress towards holding future reunions for Korean American families and their family members in North Korea.

children.heading: Consultations
children.label: 2 Consultations
children.text: The Secretary of State shall consult with the Government of the Republic of Korea, as appropriate, in carrying out this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary of State, acting through the Special Envoy on North Korean Human Rights Issues, shall include in each report required under a description of the consultations described in paragraph (2) conducted during the year preceding the submission of the report.section 7817(d) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the status of the national registry established pursuant to subsection (a)(1)(B);

children.label: I
children.text: have met their family members in North Korea during previous reunions; and

children.label: II
children.text: have yet to meet their family members in North Korea;

children.label: iii
children.text: a summary of responses by North Korea to requests by the United States Government to hold reunions of divided families; and

children.label: iv
children.text: a description of actions taken by North Korea that prevent the emigration of family members of Korean American families.

children.heading: Appropriate congressional committees defined
children.label: c Appropriate congressional committees defined
children.text: In this section, the term ‘appropriate congressional committees’ means the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate and the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives.
